revision_summary: Three targeted fixes required: (1) SCHEMA REFERENCE FIX — cherry_pick_moments[3] (ts 01:30): replace rebuttals.changed_mind with a more accurate schema reference (protocols.hello_objection_not_interested or analysis_directives.guilt_as_objection_detection). Wendy never expressed prior interest, so changed_mind is a category mismatch. Rebuild the coaching implication accordingly — the soft exit is a polite disengagement, not a reversal. (2) SCHEMA REFERENCE FIX — trajectory_verdict.outcome_dispute: replace dq_rules.ppa_existing_system with orphaned_flow hard_dq_answers: lease_with_no_buyout. Wendy describes a lease, not a PPA; the schema distinguishes these as separate entries. (3) MISSED MOMENTS — Add three cherry_pick_moments entries: (a) ts 01:15 'Do you need more panels? Is your current system not covering your electric bill?' classified as scope_creep per setter_scope_discipline — Meg asking diagnostic questions about a competitor's system performance is a distinct scope-creep instance from the 440-watt spec answer and should be flagged separately; (b) ts 01:19 'My electric bill used to be, like, $15.20 dollars, and to go from there to, like, 200 and something wasn't gonna happen' classified as bill_anchor_opportunity — this is the emotionally loaded elaboration of the bill pain with magnitude, distinct from the initial disclosure at 01:05, and the flip opportunity here is sharper; (c) ts 01:27 'So I'm not sure what's going on. But yeah.' classified as curiosity_signal — Wendy explicitly signals confusion about her own system, which is a direct curiosity-creation opening that Stage 3 treats as part of the exit sequence rather than a distinct actionable moment. OPTIONAL POLISH: Soften the tone on cherry_pick_moments[4] (ts 02:20) — the warm-exit coaching note is valid but the current framing overstates the severity of 'I'll just take you off my list' on a hard DQ call. Also correct the flipped hypothesis at ts 00:20: Meg's product-alternatives probe is better read as a misguided strategic upsell attempt (knowledge gap about DQ rules for leased customers) than pure reactive improvisation — the coaching implication shifts from 'drill the protocol sequence' to 'drill the DQ rules so the upsell probe is correctly scoped.'
